message:
perf(vdbe): replace SwissIndex HashMap with flat CursorSlots array for cursor access

V1.6: Cursor IDs are small non-negative integers (0-15 typical). Replace
SwissIndex<i32, StorageCursor/MemCursor/SorterCursor> (HashMap-based,
~10-20ns per probe: hash + probe + deref) with CursorSlots<V>
(Vec<Option<V>>, ~1ns per access: direct array index).

CursorSlots provides the same API as SwissIndex (get, get_mut, insert,
remove, clear, is_empty, len, contains_key, values, iter) but uses
flat Vec<Option<V>> storage. No hashing, no probing, no pointer chasing.

C SQLite uses p->apCsr[P1] — direct array index. This matches that pattern.

For a 7-opcode INSERT with ~4 cursor accesses per row:
  Before: 4 × 10-20ns = 40-80ns/row
  After:  4 × 1ns = 4ns/row
  Savings: ~36-76ns/row

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
